summary: Camille Fournier argues that platform teams rarely drive novel innovation, instead absorbing external innovations or internal team creations. However, this doesn't diminish the need for platform strategy—rather, platform teams serve as the fulcrum for architectural evolution across companies. She contends that platform leadership strategy involves watching industry trends and making incremental improvements that enable natural evolution. The key insight is that 'boring but reliable forward progress' is itself strategic, even if it's not revolutionary innovation.
key_points: Platform teams typically adopt external innovations or absorb platform-like work created by application teams, rather than driving novel innovation themselves
Every major technology shift (containerization, cloud, microservices, observability) has required platform-level support to succeed
Platform strategy means watching the state of the art and making tools available that encourage natural evolution, not forcing overnight transformations
'Strategy' and 'Vision' are conflated in ways that cause people to overlook steady, incremental progress as strategic work
When platform leaders execute well over years, product teams gain flexibility and power to innovate without going it alone
money_quotes: Platform teams are often the fulcrum that drives important architectural evolution across companies.
You're watching where the state of the art is going, and thinking about what makes sense to introduce or evolve so that you can take advantage of that over time.

You're not forcing everyone to move from bare metal hardware to kubernetes overnight, but you're making the tools to support bare metal to virtualization/containers available, easy to use, and cost-effective.
This is the engine of baseline modernization that needs constant feeding to keep your technology stack from stagnating.
'Strategy' and 'Vision' are so often conflated in peoples' minds that this kind of boring but reliable forward progress is overlooked as strategic.
Platform leaders, don't feel bad if you're not at the forefront of innovation!
You don't have to drive the next innovation to be strategic, you just need to make it easy for others to get there.
key_insight: Platform leadership strategy isn't about driving revolutionary innovation—it's about steady, incremental modernization that makes it easy for others to innovate.
